source.description: A reviewed six-panel raster marker contact sheet regenerated for the explicit owner-directed correction to the June 2 Doodlea.day backfill lesson, then cropped into step frames and the finished master.
source.notes: The sheet keeps one face-free cartoon stapler with no text, labels, arrows, sticker border, or badge frame. Panel 1 is deliberately pale, loose construction for the exact final silhouette; the staple slot remains visible from step 4 through the finished panel.

process_strategy: A loose pale construction drawing establishes the exact full-size stapler silhouette, then the same guide is traced and refined into the shell, base, hinge, slot, feet, marker fills, and shadow before the final only reinforces existing lines and fills.

final_elements.name: top stapler shell
final_elements.description: A rounded top shell first mapped as a pale loose guide, then traced at the same silhouette.

final_elements.name: bottom base
final_elements.description: A flat rounded base underneath the top shell, also established in the pale guide.

final_elements.name: traced keeper contour
final_elements.description: A thin black trace of the exact pale construction silhouette, with the guide still visible beneath it.

final_elements.name: front nose
final_elements.description: A squared left front end where the staple exits.

final_elements.name: rear hinge
final_elements.description: A round teal hinge cap at the right rear.

final_elements.name: staple slot and feet
final_elements.description: A dark short rounded staple slot near the left front of the upper base and tiny dark feet under the base; both stay visible through the final frame.

final_elements.name: teal and coral marker fill
final_elements.description: Teal fill on the top shell and hinge, plus coral fill on the base.

final_elements.name: purple ground shadow
final_elements.description: A small marker shadow below the stapler.

frames.visible_job: Places a deliberately pale, doubled construction guide for the final rounded top shell and flatter bottom base, plus light placement marks.
Traces the exact construction silhouette in thin black while leaving the pale guide visible underneath and preserving the shell-to-base gap.
Refines the squared left front end, cleans the contour, and adds the round rear hinge.
Adds the dark left-front staple slot and tiny base feet, which remain visibly unchanged in every later frame.
Adds teal and coral marker fills on existing stapler parts and a purple ground shadow while leaving the existing dark slot readable.

final_step.visible_job: Only thickens the existing black outlines, evens marker fills, and reinforces the same front end, hinge, dark slot, feet, and shadow without moving or covering the slot.
allowed_changes: small highlight gaps on existing color areas

rejection_checks: No major contour, prop, marking, or color choice should first appear in the final image.
The pale construction silhouette in step 1 must match the traced black contour in step 2, so the rough sketch is visibly a guide rather than a replacement shape.
The dark staple slot introduced in step 4 must remain visible in steps 5 and the finished image.
No frame may darken, ink, color, shade, or clean a feature that was not already visible in an earlier frame.
No adjacent frame should look unchanged at card size.
The final image must keep the same pose, scale, and placement as the step frames.
